compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
Implementation committed on branch worktree-jira-sync-automation (commit ce549c3a):
- rebar.toml [jira] target (url/user/project=REB)
- .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml (*/20 reconciler; mount tickets worktree; rebar reconcile --mode; exit 75/3 handling; commit-back+push when dirty OR ahead — covers the reconciler's own unpushed binding commit)
- .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge-canary.yml (hourly heartbeat; rebar-CLI bug file/close; transient-API safe; min permissions)
- docs/jira-sync-setup.md (client vars/secrets, acli pinning, reconcile-check->dry-run->live rollout, Necessary&sufficient validation, DSO divergences, rollback, optional weekly bridge-fsck)
Both workflows pass actionlint. All 8 plan-review advisories folded in (staleness window, permissions blocks, rollback docs, doc location, observable ACs).
REMAINING (deploy — needs operator): set repo Variables (JIRA_URL/USER/PROJECT, ACLI_VERSION/SHA256, optional bridge-bot/REBAR_ENV_ID) + Secret (JIRA_API_TOKEN); push branch + open PR; dispatch reconcile-check then dry-run before enabling the live schedule.

comments.author: Iso Tester
comments.body: MERGED + LIVE. Automated Jira<->rebar sync to REB is operational: reconcile-bridge.yml (*/20) + reconcile-bridge-canary.yml + docs/jira-sync-setup.md all on main (#20). Repo vars/secret wired (JIRA_URL/USER/PROJECT, ACLI_VERSION/SHA256, JIRA_API_TOKEN). Validated CI job end-to-end: reconcile-check -> dry-run (0 DIG) -> live (applied 404/404, converged). Store re-targeted from DIG to REB (bug 626d) and bulk-synced; 355 REB bindings recorded. Follow-ups: assignee churn 9b94; bulk-sync timeout raised to 60m (#23). Bridge auto-runs every 20m and is healthy.

compiled_state.description:
## Context

rebar forked from navapbc/digital-service-orchestra (DSO) and inherits DSO's
Jira-reconciler design, but rebar currently has **no CI that runs the reconciler** —
Jira sync only happens when a human runs `rebar reconcile` locally. We want the
Jira <-> rebar sync to run automatically and observably, modeled on DSO's two
GitHub Actions workflows (`reconcile-bridge.yml` + `reconcile-bridge-canary.yml`),
translated to rebar's diverged surface, and documented so client projects can adopt
it from templates we ship.

### Confirmed divergences from DSO (drive the translation)
- Invocation: `rebar reconcile --mode live` (== `python -m rebar_reconciler`).
  Exit 75 = reschedule (re-run, NOT failure); exit 3 = another pass in flight.
- **Reconciler does NOT push its event writes.** It writes inbound events as
  uncommitted files into the `.tickets-tracker` worktree and bypasses the CLI
  auto-push path, so a CI **commit-back + push to `origin/tickets`** step is
  REQUIRED (DSO's loop is necessary, not redundant). `REBAR_SYNC_PUSH` does not
  cover this.
- No `BRIDGE_ENV_ID`; optional `REBAR_ENV_ID` (default author stamp "reconciler").
- Env names are the new `REBAR_*` set; Jira creds stay `JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/
  JIRA_PROJECT` (vars) + `JIRA_API_TOKEN` (secret, env-only).
- Canary ticket ops -> `rebar list --has-tag --output json`, `rebar create bug
  ... --priority --tags --description`, `rebar comment`, `rebar transition <id>
  open closed --reason "Fixed: ..."` (bug-close reason prefix enforced).
- Do NOT wire `scripts/jira-pressure-test/` e2e probes into CI (explicitly manual,
  live-mutating). Prefer `rebar bridge-probe` / `--mode reconcile-check`.

## Success Criteria

- A scheduled GitHub Actions workflow keeps the REB Jira project and the rebar
  `tickets` store in sync automatically on a ~20-minute cadence, with single-flight
  concurrency and correct exit-code handling.
- A heartbeat canary detects when the reconciler goes stale and files/auto-closes a
  rebar bug ticket, giving operators a visible failure signal.
- The set of workflows + repo vars/secrets is validated as NECESSARY and SUFFICIENT
  for an automated, durable, bidirectional sync (no silent event loss; events are
  committed back and pushed).
- Client projects can stand up the same sync by copying templates and following a
  setup doc — no need to reverse-engineer the workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`: schedule (~*/20) + workflow_dispatch
      (mode choice incl. dry-run/reconcile-check/live); mounts the `tickets` orphan
      branch as `.tickets-tracker` worktree; installs rebar + pinned acli (checksum
      verified) and authenticates; runs `rebar reconcile --mode <mode>`; treats exit
      75 as reschedule and 3 as benign "pass in flight"; commits reconciler events
      back and pushes to `origin/tickets` via a fetch-rebase-push retry loop; single-
      flight `concurrency`; failure surfaced to the run summary.
- [ ] `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge-canary.yml`: hourly schedule; checks the
      last successful `reconcile-bridge.yml` run via the Actions API; when stale
      beyond the window, opens (or comments on) a `heartbeat-alert`-tagged bug via
      the `rebar` CLI and auto-closes it on recovery; treats API errors as transient
      (no false alarms); commits ticket changes back to `origin/tickets`.
- [ ] Necessary+sufficient validation written up: each workflow step justified
      against rebar's actual reconciler surface (commit-back required, exit codes,
      lock semantics, creds), with anything intentionally omitted called out.
- [ ] `rebar.toml` carries the `[jira]` sync target (url/user/project=REB); the
      `JIRA_API_TOKEN` secret stays env-only.
- [ ] A setup doc (e.g. `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`) documents, for a CLIENT project,
      exactly which repo **variables** (JIRA_URL/JIRA_USER/JIRA_PROJECT, ACLI_VERSION/
      ACLI_SHA256, bridge-bot identity) and **secrets** (JIRA_API_TOKEN) to set, how
      to mount the tickets branch, and how to copy the workflow templates; the two
      workflow files double as the copy-paste templates.
- [ ] Workflows lint/parse (actionlint or equivalent) and a dry-run/`reconcile-check`
      dispatch path is described for safe first validation before enabling `live`.

## Plan-review remediations (advisory findings triaged — all valid)

- **Canary staleness threshold (fd3a, E2).** Pin the window: alert when no
  successful `reconcile-bridge.yml` run completed in the **last 60 minutes**
  (3x the 20-min cadence); exposed as a `workflow_dispatch` override
  (`alert_window_hours`, default 2 to match DSO) and validated as a positive int.
- **Necessary+sufficient write-up location (f67d/T7, F1).** It lives as a
  **`## Necessary & sufficient` section inside `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`** — ONE
  doc, not a separate design doc (resolves the navigation-ambiguity finding). The
  section maps each workflow step to the rebar reconciler fact that requires it.
- **Rollback / disable path (f8f1, T4).** Document: disable via Actions UI (toggle
  the workflow) or remove the `schedule:` trigger; an erroneous `origin/tickets`
  push is revertable via normal git history on the orphan branch; the
  `dry-run` / `reconcile-check` dispatch mode is the mandatory safe-first-validation
  gate before enabling `live`.
- **Least-privilege token + identity (f638, T10).** Each workflow pins an explicit
  minimal `permissions:` block — reconcile-bridge: `contents: write`; canary:
  `contents: write` + `actions: read`. The default `GITHUB_TOKEN` is sufficient for
  both the `origin/tickets` push and the Actions API read (no PAT needed); the
  commit identity is a configurable bridge-bot (`BRIDGE_BOT_NAME`/`_EMAIL` repo vars).
- **AC-quality / observable outcomes (f607, P6).** "Sufficient" is operationalized:
  the necessary+sufficient claim is checkable as the `docs/jira-sync-setup.md`
  section that justifies every step against a cited reconciler fact (commit-back
  required, exit 75/3 handling, lock semantics, env/creds), with omissions listed.
compiled_state.title: Automated Jira<->rebar sync via GitHub Actions (reconcile-bridge + heartbeat canary) + client setup docs
